clusters.title: Major earthquake in Philippines kills at least 16, spawns yard-high tsunami

items.title: Major earthquake in Philippines kills at least 16, spawns yard-high tsunami
items.standfirst: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines, killing at least 16 and sparking a 3-foot tsunami along nearby coasts, officials said.
items.content: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines, killing at least 16 and sparking a 3-foot tsunami along nearby coasts, officials said.

items.title: At least 15 killed as powerful earthquake strikes southern Philippines
items.standfirst: Residents warned not to enter damaged homes or other buildings owing to threat of aftershocks after 7.8-magnitude quakeA magnitude-7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early on Monday, collapsing buildings and killing at least 15 people.“Many buildings were affected, but I cannot enumerate them now because we are busy with ongoing rescues,” Robert Dagon, of the General Santos city police, told Agence France-Presse. Continue reading...
items.content: Residents warned not to enter damaged homes or other buildings owing to threat of aftershocks after 7.8-magnitude quakeA magnitude-7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early on Monday, collapsing buildings and killing at least 15 people.“Many buildings were affected, but I cannot enumerate them now because we are busy with ongoing rescues,” Robert Dagon, of the General Santos city police, told Agence France-Presse. Continue reading...

items.title: Major quake off Philippines kills at least 15, triggers tsunami warnings
items.standfirst: A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake rocked the southern Philippines on Monday, killing at least 15 people, toppling buildings and prompting tsunami warnings across parts of Southeast Asia and the Pacific, as authorities ordered coastal residents to evacuate and rescuers searched for victims amid strong aftershocks.
items.content: A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake rocked the southern Philippines on Monday, killing at least 15 people, toppling buildings and prompting tsunami warnings across parts of Southeast Asia and the Pacific, as authorities ordered coastal residents to evacuate and rescuers searched for victims amid strong aftershocks.

items.title: A 7.8 magnitude quake in the Philippines kills at least 16
items.standfirst: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines Monday, killing at least 16 people, injuring more than 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sending a 3-foot tsunami into nearby coasts.
items.content: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines Monday, killing at least 16 people, injuring more than 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sending a 3-foot tsunami into nearby coasts.(Image credit: Philippine Red Cross via AP)

items.title: Powerful earthquake hits Philippines, killing at least 15
items.standfirst: Tsunami threat recedes after authorities' warnings following magnitude 7.8 earthquake off island of Mindanao.
items.content: Tsunami threat recedes after authorities' warnings following magnitude 7.8 earthquake off island of Mindanao.

items.title: Tsunami alert and at least 16 dead after earthquake hits the Philippines
items.standfirst: Tsunami alerts have been issued and at least 16 people have died after a magnitude-7.8 earthquake struck the island of Mindanao in the Philippines.
items.content: Tsunami alerts have been issued and at least 16 people have died after a magnitude-7.8 earthquake struck the island of Mindanao in the Philippines.

ai_summary: At least 16 people killed in the earthquake
7.8 magnitude earthquake struck the southern Philippines
3-foot tsunami hit nearby coasts
Over 200 people injured in the disaster
Rescue efforts are currently underway

clusters.title: Armenia’s pro-Europe party wins election and cements shift away from Russia
clusters.neutral_headline: Armenia Election Results Confirm Shift Away From Russia

items.title: Armenia’s pro-Europe party wins election and cements shift away from Russia
items.standfirst: Result strengthens PM Nikol Pashinyan’s drive for deeper integration with Europe despite warnings from MoscowArmenia’s ruling pro-Europe party has won parliamentary elections, confirming the country’s pivot towards Europe and away from its traditional ally, Russia.Final results in the small South Caucasus country showed the prime minister Nikol Pashinyan’s Civil Contract party securing a slim majority, while the Strong Armenia alliance, led by the Russian-Armenian billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, won 25% of the seats in parliament. Continue reading...
items.content: Result strengthens PM Nikol Pashinyan’s drive for deeper integration with Europe despite warnings from MoscowArmenia’s ruling pro-Europe party has won parliamentary elections, confirming the country’s pivot towards Europe and away from its traditional ally, Russia.Final results in the small South Caucasus country showed the prime minister Nikol Pashinyan’s Civil Contract party securing a slim majority, while the Strong Armenia alliance, led by the Russian-Armenian billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, won 25% of the seats in parliament. Continue reading...

items.title: Armenia's parliamentary election a vote on nation's 'geopolitical future'
items.standfirst: Armenians voted on Sunday in a parliamentary election seen as a test of the government's efforts to forge a peace deal following a crushing military defeat by Azerbaijan three years ago. "This is really a vote on the geopolitical future of Armenia and its ties with Russia," FRANCE 24's Philip Turle said in an analysis. He pointed to Armenia's shift away from Russia, a trend that dates back to its defeat by Azerbaijan and was accelerated by the war in Ukraine, which has diverted Russia's attention elsewhere.
items.content: Armenians voted on Sunday in a parliamentary election seen as a test of the government's efforts to forge a peace deal following a crushing military defeat by Azerbaijan three years ago. "This is really a vote on the geopolitical future of Armenia and its ties with Russia," FRANCE 24's Philip Turle said in an analysis. He pointed to Armenia's shift away from Russia, a trend that dates back to its defeat by Azerbaijan and was accelerated by the war in Ukraine, which has diverted Russia's attention elsewhere.

items.title: Armenians vote in general election watched closely by Russia and the West
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan and his governing party are looking for a strong mandate for a new geopolitical course for Armenia. The opposition includes some parties that are vocally pro-Russia.
items.content: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan and his governing party are looking for a strong mandate for a new geopolitical course for Armenia. The opposition includes some parties that are vocally pro-Russia.(Image credit: Anthony Pizzoferrato)

items.title: Armenia votes in test of PM Pashinyan's pivot away from Russia
items.standfirst: Armenians voted in a parliamentary election on Sunday as the incumbent government, under mounting Russian pressure, sought to loosen ties with Moscow and deepen cooperation with the West. Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan and his governing Civil Contract party are looking for a strong mandate for a new geopolitical course for Armenia. The opposition they face includes some parties that are vocally pro-Russia. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot reports from Yerevan.
items.content: Armenians voted in a parliamentary election on Sunday as the incumbent government, under mounting Russian pressure, sought to loosen ties with Moscow and deepen cooperation with the West. Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan and his governing Civil Contract party are looking for a strong mandate for a new geopolitical course for Armenia. The opposition they face includes some parties that are vocally pro-Russia. FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot reports from Yerevan.

ai_summary: Armenia's pro-Europe party wins parliamentary election
Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's party secures slim majority
Strong Armenia alliance wins 25 percent of seats
Election results confirm Armenia's pivot towards Europe
Russia's influence in Armenia continues to decline

clusters.title: French Open: Zverev defeats Cobolli to claim first Grand Slam title
clusters.neutral_headline: Alexander Zverev Wins French Open Title

items.title: French Open: Zverev defeats Cobolli to claim first Grand Slam title
items.standfirst: Alexander Zverev won the French Open final against Flavio Cobolli. He wins first career grand slam title.
items.content: Alexander Zverev won the French Open final against Flavio Cobolli. He wins first career grand slam title.

items.title: Alexander Zverev wins the French Open to finally earn a 1st Grand Slam title
items.standfirst: After Italian Flavio Cobolli missed an overhead on the second championship point of the five-set encounter, Zverev dropped on his back and began sobbing.
items.content: After Italian Flavio Cobolli missed an overhead on the second championship point of the five-set encounter, Zverev dropped on his back and began sobbing. (Image credit: Aurelien Morissard)

items.title: Alexander Zverev becomes Grand Slam champion with French Open win
items.standfirst: In his fourth major final, Alexander Zverev beat Flavio Cobolli 6-1, 4-6, 6-4, 6-7 (5), 6-1 for the French Open title on Sunday.
items.content: In his fourth major final, Alexander Zverev beat Flavio Cobolli 6-1, 4-6, 6-4, 6-7 (5), 6-1 for the French Open title on Sunday.

items.title: French Open: Zverev beats Cobolli to win first Grand Slam title
items.standfirst: Alexander Zverev finally secured his maiden Grand Slam title with a dramatic five-set victory over Italy's Flavio Cobolli in the French Open final on Sunday.
items.content: Alexander Zverev finally secured his maiden Grand Slam title with a dramatic five-set victory over Italy's Flavio Cobolli in the French Open final on Sunday.

ai_summary: Alexander Zverev wins first Grand Slam title
Zverev defeats Flavio Cobolli in the French Open final
Zverev wins the match in five sets
Zverev becomes the new French Open champion
Zverev's victory marks a major milestone in his career

clusters.title: Israel and Iran trade strikes, threatening to drag region back to full-scale war

items.title: Israel and Iran trade strikes, threatening to drag region back to full-scale war
items.standfirst: Israel and Iran traded fire early Monday in retaliatory strikes that threatened to drag the wider Middle East back into a full-scale war, while Yemen's Houthi rebels warned they would target Israel-affiliated ships in the Red Sea.
items.content: Israel and Iran traded fire early Monday in retaliatory strikes that threatened to drag the wider Middle East back into a full-scale war, while Yemen's Houthi rebels warned they would target Israel-affiliated ships in the Red Sea.(Image credit: Rami Shlush)

items.title: Middle East war live: Israel and Iran trade air strikes, threatening to drag region back to war
items.standfirst: Israel and Iran traded strikes early Israel on Monday in retaliatory attacks that threatened to drag the Middle East back into a regional war. Israeli authorities warned that two waves of Iranian missiles were targeting the country, and called for the public to seek shelter. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Israel and Iran traded strikes early Israel on Monday in retaliatory attacks that threatened to drag the Middle East back into a regional war. Israeli authorities warned that two waves of Iranian missiles were targeting the country, and called for the public to seek shelter. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

ai_summary: Israel and Iran engage in retaliatory strikes
Strikes threaten to escalate into full-scale war
Israel warns of two waves of Iranian missiles
Public advised to seek shelter in Israel
Tensions between Israel and Iran continue to rise

clusters.title: Ten dead after migrant boat capsizes near Malta, Italian coastguard says

items.title: Ten dead after migrant boat capsizes near Malta, Italian coastguard says
items.standfirst: At least 10 bodies have been recovered after a migrant boat carrying about 60 people capsized near Malta , the Italian coastguard says.
items.content: At least 10 bodies have been recovered after a migrant boat carrying about 60 people capsized near Malta , the Italian coastguard says.

items.title: Italian rescuers recover 10 bodies after migrant boat capsizes off Malta
items.standfirst: About 48 people rescued alive after vessel reportedly left Libya carrying about 60 passengersItalian rescuers have recovered 10 bodies after a migrant boat capsized in waters off Malta, a coastguard statement said on Sunday.The vessel, which had departed from Libya carrying about 60 people, overturned about 45 nautical miles east-south-east of Malta, the Italian coastguard said. Continue reading...
items.content: About 48 people rescued alive after vessel reportedly left Libya carrying about 60 passengersItalian rescuers have recovered 10 bodies after a migrant boat capsized in waters off Malta, a coastguard statement said on Sunday.The vessel, which had departed from Libya carrying about 60 people, overturned about 45 nautical miles east-south-east of Malta, the Italian coastguard said. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump walks out of interview with NBC’s Meet the Press after clash over election claims
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump Walks Out of NBC Interview

items.title: Trump walks out of interview with NBC’s Meet the Press after clash over election claims
items.standfirst: Kristen Welker questioned Trump’s allegations that races for California governor and 2020 president were ‘rigged’Donald Trump walked out of an interview with NBC’s Meet the Press after he repeatedly made false claims that the US 2020 presidential elections was rigged in 2020 and questions around compensation for those charged in the January 6 insurrection.The US president’s abrupt exit came during a tense exchange between himself and NBC’s Kristen Welker during a Friday interview in Wisconsin that aired on Sunday. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.content: During the interview, the president was repeatedly challenged on several points by the show's presenter Kristen Welker.

clusters.title: European leaders back Zelensky's call for direct Russia talks
clusters.neutral_headline: European Leaders Support Zelensky's Call for Talks

items.title: European leaders back Zelensky's call for direct Russia talks
items.standfirst: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky won support from the leaders of Britain, France and Germany on Sunday for direct ceasefire talks with Russia, as diplomatic efforts to end the war remain stalled. The appeal came as Russian strikes reportedly damaged a nuclear fuel storage facility near the Chernobyl exclusion zone.
items.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky won support from the leaders of Britain, France and Germany on Sunday for direct ceasefire talks with Russia, as diplomatic efforts to end the war remain stalled. The appeal came as Russian strikes reportedly damaged a nuclear fuel storage facility near the Chernobyl exclusion zone.

items.title: Zelenskyy discusses ‘urgent need to scale up’ air defences with key allies in London
items.standfirst: Keir Starmer hosts Ukrainian, French and German leaders in Downing Street after Russia fires hypersonic weapons at UkraineVolodymyr Zelenskyy and the leaders of the UK, France and Germany discussed “the urgent need to scale up” Ukraine’s air defences and deep-strike capabilities in London on Sunday night, after Russia fired hypersonic weapons at Ukraine, Downing Street said.The meeting of Ukraine’s staunchest allies in London came hours after a Russian drone strike damaged a storage centre for spent nuclear fuel nine miles from the Chornobyl nuclear power plant. Continue reading...
items.content: Keir Starmer hosts Ukrainian, French and German leaders in Downing Street after Russia fires hypersonic weapons at UkraineVolodymyr Zelenskyy and the leaders of the UK, France and Germany discussed “the urgent need to scale up” Ukraine’s air defences and deep-strike capabilities in London on Sunday night, after Russia fired hypersonic weapons at Ukraine, Downing Street said.The meeting of Ukraine’s staunchest allies in London came hours after a Russian drone strike damaged a storage centre for spent nuclear fuel nine miles from the Chornobyl nuclear power plant. Continue reading...

items.title: Russian drone hits building storing spent nuclear fuel near Chornobyl
items.standfirst: Attack was ‘extremely vile’ and deliberate, says Ukraine’s president Volodymyr Zelenskyy A Russian Shahed drone has substantially damaged a building used to store spent nuclear fuel close to the disused Chornobyl nuclear power plant, in what Ukraine’s president described as a deliberate and “extremely vile” attack.While the structure – the reception building of the spent fuel storage facility – was empty of containers at the time, the targeting of the sensitive site appeared to be direct messaging from Moscow amid an intensifying battle of long-range aerial strikes in which high-profile locations on both sides have been hit. Continue reading...
items.content: Attack was ‘extremely vile’ and deliberate, says Ukraine’s president Volodymyr Zelenskyy A Russian Shahed drone has substantially damaged a building used to store spent nuclear fuel close to the disused Chornobyl nuclear power plant, in what Ukraine’s president described as a deliberate and “extremely vile” attack.While the structure – the reception building of the spent fuel storage facility – was empty of containers at the time, the targeting of the sensitive site appeared to be direct messaging from Moscow amid an intensifying battle of long-range aerial strikes in which high-profile locations on both sides have been hit. Continue reading...

ai_summary: European leaders back Zelensky's call for direct talks
Talks aim to end the war between Ukraine and Russia
Russian strikes damage nuclear fuel storage facility
Ukraine's air defenses and deep-strike capabilities discussed
Meeting between Ukraine and European leaders held in London
